source: companiesmarketcap.com/usa — top 100 US companies by market cap as of April 9, 2026
_description: The CFO Silvia content universe. The event detector uses this to decide what to cover. No more subjective judgment on what counts as 'Silvia-caliber.'

tier_1_auto_cover._rule: Auto-produce a post for every earnings report and every major event from these tickers. No judgment call. If they report, we write.

_notes.GOOGL: Alphabet Class A. GOOG (Class C) is the same underlying company; cover as Alphabet.
_notes.BRK.B: Berkshire Hathaway Class B. BRK.A also exists but B is more commonly referenced.
_notes.GEV: GE Vernova, spun off from GE in 2024. Now a mega cap.
_notes.SNDK: SanDisk, spun off from Western Digital in 2025.
_notes.MU: Micron Technology. High on the list due to AI memory demand. Silvia-worthy.

tier_2_conditional_sp500._rule: All other S&P 500 members. Cover ONLY if at least ONE of the conditions below is met. This keeps us from drowning in 500 posts per earnings season.

eps_beat_or_miss_pct.description: Covered if (actual_EPS - consensus) / |consensus| is >= 5% beat or <= -5% miss

revenue_beat_or_miss_pct.description: Covered if revenue beats/misses consensus by >= 3%

stock_move_pct.description: Covered if post-earnings stock moves >= 3% in pre-market or after-hours

tier_3_conditional_non_sp._rule: Non-S&P 500 names. Cover only for extraordinary reasons. Default is SKIP. Silvia is not a meme-stock account.

stock_move_pct.description: Non-S&P names need a much bigger move than Tier 2 to justify attention. 8%+ intraday on a concrete catalyst, not on momentum.

allowlist_recurring_tier_3._description: Non-S&P names we will cover for earnings or major events even without a breakout catalyst. These are 'almost Tier 1' — high-profile enough that Silvia's audience expects coverage.
allowlist_recurring_tier_3._rationale: These names are either (a) household names to @CFOSilvia's retail-leaning audience or (b) volatile enough that ignoring a big move would miss the story. Covered with the same rigor as Tier 2: earnings trigger auto-coverage, other events need a >3% move or narrative trigger.

earnings.tier_2: cover if EPS beat/miss 5%+, revenue beat/miss 3%+, stock move 3%+, or any narrative trigger
earnings.tier_3_other: cover only if stock move 8%+ with a qualifying catalyst

macro_releases.conditional_rule: Cover if the print deviates from consensus by more than 10% on the headline figure, OR if it is accompanied by a major market reaction (2%+ index move).

fed_speakers.conditional: Regional Fed presidents only if they explicitly signal a policy pivot or dissent from the last FOMC statement.

geopolitical_or_surprise.rule: Cover if the event moves any major index >1% or oil/gold/DXY >3% intraday. Also cover if it names a Tier 1 sector specifically (energy war, tech tariffs, bank regulation).

daily_defaults.daily_wrap_content: session summary, indices, commodities, top earnings, key stories, tomorrow's preview

operator_notes.review_cadence: Refresh this file quarterly or after a major index rebalance. Track S&P 500 additions/removals from the S&P website.
operator_notes.edit_rule: Add to tier_1_auto_cover only if a name has crossed $100B market cap AND has the kind of retail recognition that warrants always-cover. Do not auto-add S&P additions.
